editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter to Edward Carson (1854-1935). Carson was a prominent Unionist Politician. Originally a practicing solicitor from Dublin, Carson's opposition to the proposed third Home Rule bill made him the figure head of Unionism throughout the island. He was the centerpiece of the speaking tour that culminated in Ulster Day (28 September 1912), when just under half a million signed Ulster's solemn league and covenant pledging to use ‘all means which may be found necessary to defeat the present conspiracy to set up a home rule parliament in Dublin’. Carson inaugurated the Larne gunrunning in 1914 which armed the UVF, many of whom Carson would encourage to join the British Army throughout the First World War.

msIdentifier.repository: Public Record Office of Northern Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Confidential TELEPHONE 2794. 34 14thOct
30 UPPER PEMBROKE STREET,
DUBLIN D1507/A/19/12 My dear Ned The situation here
is growing more dangerous every
day and I greatly fear that
Duke is unable or unwilling to
tackle it. You will under-
stand the situation better
if you read the enclosed which
is a copy of a letter I have
just written him. He is
desperately slow in making
up his mind & very weak.
To give you true illustrations
Hue and Cry for months was arrested a week
ago. I advised and Gen. Maxwell agreed with
me that he should be handed over & tried
by Court-Martial, as Civil Trials are at
present suspended in the case of all
offences against Defence of Realm Regulations.
Duke has now directed that this man
should be had summarily before the
Magistrates for unlawful assembly, but
I have protested against this as a farce
in the case of a man guilty of high treason
35 A man named Athy who was
the ringleader of every form of
lawlessness in one of the worst
parts of Co. Galway, led the
party who on Easter Monday
attacked the Oranmore Police
Barracks & fired upon the
police inside. Most of the
party were afterwards arrested
& got four years penal servitude
from Courts Martial
on charges under Defence of
Realm Regulations. This man
Athy absconded and after
being on the run & on the
36 again I prepared many weeks
ago elaborate directions to the
Police as to seizing seditious
publications, dispersing meetings
seizing disloyal pamphlets,
badges etc but I cannot get
him to express his approval
& meanwhile the Police are at
their wits end for want of a
lead. All this is private of
course but I thought you would
like to know how the land lies.
I hope to be over for the Irish
debate on Wednesday Yours ever.
James Don't refer in the House
to what I mention to
Duke about the
rather assurance
come about his dinner
